markdown_projection.note: JSON contract is authority. No markdown projection is generated for WP-KERNEL-009 (prevents projection drift).

scope.scope: Confirm Tiptap package presence and define extension ownership, schema versioning, custom nodes, collaboration hooks, and tests. This MT belongs to NativeDependencyAndPackaging. Make WP-009 native-by-default and fully packaged inside Handshake.
constraints: Use the existing microtask_plan_draft execution surface only; do not introduce a separate MT execution surface or suite field.

Core Handshake behavior must be Handshake-native: no outside app, outside server, external daemon, Docker-only path, SQLite fallback, Redis/Valkey cache authority, or sidecar paper as proof or authority.

Allowed external runtime inputs are limited to operator-provided models, GGUF/model files, tensors/safetensors, and CUI portable artifacts where the owning WP explicitly gates them; Tiptap and Monaco are allowed only as bundled/lockfile-governed product libraries integrated into Handshake.

PostgreSQL plus EventLedger is canonical for durable state, receipts, indexing evidence, runtime proof, and validation; generated markdown, wiki pages, HTML, context bundles, debug reports, and UI projections are projections only.
Every implementation handoff must be restartable by a no-context local model using only the WP/MT contract, current Master Spec bundle, product code, tests, and receipts.
No proof may require manually launched external services or tools except explicitly allowed model/GGUF/tensor/CUI-portable surfaces.
Bundled frontend/editor libraries must be lockfile-governed, license-reviewed, and callable through Handshake UI/runtime surfaces.
scope.expected_behavior: TiptapPackageIntegrationPlan leaves a concrete, restartable implementation or activation artifact with PostgreSQL/EventLedger authority, Master Spec anchors, UserManual impact, and validator-readable evidence.
acceptance_criteria: TiptapPackageIntegrationPlan is implemented or specified with enough detail for a no-context local model to continue from the MT alone.
The MT names exact product/governance files, state tables or APIs, constraints, failure modes, and verification evidence.
The MT rejects sidecar research-paper authority and outside-runtime proof; accepted research ideas are restated as Handshake-native requirements.
The MT handoff names HBR rows proven, blocked, or not applicable with concrete reasons.
verification: Parse the WP/MT contract and confirm this MT keeps all required fields populated.
Run the smallest relevant product, governance, or fixture test available for the touched surface; if not implemented yet, define the exact future test and negative case.
For UI/editor/UserManual surfaces, use visual debugging or declare why no GUI surface exists for this MT.
Confirm no SQLite, Docker-default, external-app, external-daemon, sidecar-paper, or mock-only proof satisfies the MT.
handoff_requirements: List files changed or intentionally left untouched.
List commands/tests run and exact pass/fail/blocker status.
List EventLedger/PostgreSQL evidence, receipt IDs, fixtures, visual-debug artifacts, and repair queues produced or expected.
List remaining risks, compatibility shims, deprecated wording, and next MTs unblocked.

lifecycle.implementation_evidence: Implemented in wtc-kernel-009 feat/WP-KERNEL-009 commit c76c5fbc; proof commands green per delegate report (vitest/cargo/playwright; MT-032 aggregate check:dependency-policy 8/8 pass, app suite 313/313). Pending adversarial code review before READY_FOR_VALIDATION.

concerns: buildWp009ExtensionSet uses real @tiptap/* 3.13.0 (TableKit, TaskList/Item, Mention x2) with per-factory guards; StarterKit failure is fatal (rethrown), optional extensions degrade. jsdom schema+roundtrip smoke + offline harness exercise the full set.
Extension instantiation proven via editor.extensionManager.extensions in the harness (tiptapExtensions>5 asserted offline).
evasion_probes: Mention failure injected via throwing getter on options.mentionItems flows through the REAL guarded factory (extension_init typed failure), proven in DependencyFailureBanner.test.tsx.

wp_validator_review_v1.review_scope: Validated only MTs that were READY_FOR_VALIDATION in WP-KERNEL-009; no ACP used.

findings.summary: Tiptap package/schema basics are present, but the MT contract calls for schema versioning and collaboration hooks; the Tiptap extension owner does not import or own the collaboration extension/hook surface.
findings.required_remediation: Add the missing schema-version surface and collaboration hook integration/ownership evidence, or narrow the MT contract through the orchestrator before resubmitting. Include a test proving the extension set exposes the required collaboration/version contract rather than only locking the dependency.

evidence_basis: Authoritative MT JSON contract and current product tree inspection.
Dependency failures use deterministic packet status/dependency reads only; WIP MT work products were not inspected.
remediation_summary: Add the missing schema-version surface and collaboration hook integration/ownership evidence, or narrow the MT contract through the orchestrator before resubmitting. Include a test proving the extension set exposes the required collaboration/version contract rather than only locking the dependency.

remediation_v2.fail_verdict: FAIL_V1: extension set only locked the dependency, did not expose schema-version or collaboration-hook contract
remediation_v2.remediation: Added WP009_RICH_DOCUMENT_SCHEMA_VERSION schema-version surface + Collaboration extension wired to a Yjs doc in app/src/lib/tiptap/extension_set.ts; new 6-test vitest proves the contract is exposed (editor drives the Yjs fragment), not just the dep locked. tsc/eslint clean, 16/16 no regression.

wp_validator_review_v2.review_method: Integration Validator multi-agent workflow (wf_0ac25815-485 + wf_98386044-70a): independent per-MT reviewer + adversarial confirm on FAIL. No ACP. Governance/repo paperwork non-blocking per operator directive (2026-06-18). Bar = good code vs MT contract + WP packet + current Master Spec v02.194, plus the WP_VALIDATOR Spec-Realism Gate.
wp_validator_review_v2.summary: MT-021 requires the WP-009 Tiptap extension-set owner to expose a RichDocument schema-version surface and a live collaboration-hook binding (not merely lock the dependency). The remediation_v2 commit 8005de70 implements both with real @tiptap/extension-collaboration + yjs runtime, and a jsdom test proves editor content round-trips into the provided Yjs doc. The prior FAIL_V1 gap (TIPTAP_SCHEMA_COLLAB_HOOK_GAP) is genuinely closed.
findings: FAIL_V1 (TIPTAP_SCHEMA_COLLAB_HOOK_GAP) is now closed. app/src/lib/tiptap/extension_set.ts:54 exports WP009_RICH_DOCUMENT_SCHEMA_VERSION and :219 richDocumentSchemaVersionOf() — the schema-version surface the validator required.

Collaboration hook is real and live: extension_set.ts:149-154 pushes Collaboration.configure({document: collaborationDoc}) and :108 disables StarterKit undoRedo so the Yjs CRDT owns history (documented Tiptap pattern). buildWp009CollaborativeExtensionSet(ydoc) at :200 is the public binder.

Proof exercises real libraries, not implementer mocks: extension_set_collaboration.test.ts:95-105 writes editor content via ed.commands.setContent then asserts ydoc.getXmlFragment('default').toString() contains 'collab-bound-MT-021' — a genuine @tiptap+yjs round-trip. :89-90 reads the real ed.storage.collaboration.isDisabled API proving the extension initialized.

Dependencies are genuine and lockfile-governed: app/package.json:30 @tiptap/extension-collaboration ^3.13.0, :45 yjs ^13.6.27; app/pnpm-lock.yaml:1096/3951 resolve extension-collaboration@3.13.0 and pull @tiptap/y-tiptap@3.0.1 + y-protocols transitively. No vibecoded imports.
No deferred-live escape: grep of impl + test for todo!/unimplemented/'not yet wired'/'deferred to follow'/'pending MT-'/placeholder/stub = NONE.

No forbidden deps in app/src/lib/tiptap/ (no SQLite/Elasticsearch/Docker/etc.); MT correctly scopes durable authority to WriteBox/EventLedger in other MTs (extension_set.ts:68-71, :194-196) and makes no PostgreSQL durable-authority claim itself, so sub_rule_2's PG requirement does not bind — the required Handshake-owned touch (editor/CRDT round-trip) is exercised against real libs.

Spec alignment confirmed: master-spec-v02.194/02-system-architecture.md:47 (Yjs CRDT engine), :1583 LM-LINK-002 (@mentions inline in ProseMirror/Tiptap), :722/:873 (schema_version pattern). File unchanged since the remediation commit; current HEAD state matches.

Self-cert note (sub_rule_3): claimed_by==ready_by==KERNEL_BUILDER, but the IV/WP-validator signs COMPLETED and the proof is real, so this is not a fail. Aside (non-blocking): MT lifecycle still reads FAIL_V1 / READY_FOR_VALIDATION_V2 — paperwork lag, expected, not a basis for any verdict.
evidence_checked: git show --stat 8005de70 and git log --grep MT-021

minimum_controls: No placeholders, mocks, scaffolding, or deferred-live escapes; runtime proof against Handshake-managed PostgreSQL/EventLedger where the contract names durable state.
